claim: Python 3.13 is the latest stable release
notes: Page-aware mode should recover from a thin snippet by reading the official release page.

claim: Example product launch happened this week
notes: Conflicting evidence should remain visible instead of being flattened into certainty.

claim: Example corrected statement is still true
notes: Conflict-heavy evidence should outweigh weak or missing support.

claim: Python 3.13 is the current stable release
notes: Two high-quality supporting sources should clear the supported threshold without page fetches.
One strong official source should land in likely-supported rather than supported.

claim: VendorX finalized its acquisition on March 17 2026
notes: Vague rumor snippets should not be upgraded into a confident verdict.

claim: Example corrected statement is still true
notes: Page-aware mode should convert an ambiguous snippet into a likely-false verdict when the page contains a correction.

claim: Python 3.13 remains the stable release line
notes: A single medium-to-high quality searxng result should remain likely-supported rather than supported.
Support and conflict from two providers should remain visibly contested.

claim: Python 3.13 is still the supported stable branch
notes: Page-aware mode plus a second support source should clear the supported threshold.

claim: VendorX signed definitive merger documents on March 17 2026
notes: A weakly related source should stay under the evidence threshold.

claim: Example statement remains accurate after correction
notes: Two provider-backed correction sources should sustain a likely-false verdict.
